The Holiday Cash Injection
Back when I was running my supplement company, Patriot Health Alliance,
we stumbled upon a special 4-day promotion on major holidays that ended
up making us around $2 million in pure profit the first year we did it.
At the time this was like 40% of our profit for the year.
Let that sink in.
40% of our profit for the year came from these little holiday sales that
I’m about to share with you.
- New years
- Valentine’s day
- St Patrick’s day
- Memorial Day
- 4th of July
- Labor Day
- Veterans Day
- Black Friday
- your birthday, anniversary of the company
- scratch & dent sale
- excess inventory sale
- any other holiday/special day you can think of
Let’s dig into how these holiday sales work...
There’s basically two ways to do it…
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 30 --

1. If you have a consumable product (like a supplement, skin cream, or
survival food) then you can do the holiday sale for that product
because leads will buy it and past customers will buy it.
2. The second option is to choose any other product of yours that is
popular, and put it on sale (this can be an info product, a course, a
physical product etc…)
Here’s a real example of how the holiday sale works…
As I’m writing this, it’s close to St Patrick’s Day. So we would do a 4-day
sale over St Patrick’s Day.
Usually we’ll start the sale two days before St. Patty’s Day. And then the
sale would end the day after St Patty’s day. So 4 days total.
This will all be promoted to your email list.
So over the course of the 4-day sale, we would send 10 emails (at least
two per day, and then 4 emails on the last day). This is crucial because
most of your sales will come on the first day and at the end of the last day
before the sale ends.
For the sale we’ll be selling our Patriot Power Greens supplement.
This supplement works well for this sale for a few reasons…
1. Most of our list (more than 80%) had bought the supplement already,
so it’s a great incentive to re-order the supplement or to simply
stock-up when they’re getting a deal
2. If they haven’t bought the supplement yet, this is a good opportunity
to give it a try
So that’s what we’re selling….
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 30 --

Now here’s how it works…
You simply send people from your email list to the holiday sales page.
In terms of the sales page, the easiest way to do this is to simply
create a clone of your current sales page and put a header on it
announcing the sale (i.e. something with four leaf clovers and
leprachauns for St Patty’s day). See the example below…
That’s all you have to do.
You don’t have to rewrite the copy.
You don’t have to do any special videos.
Keep everything else the same in terms of the checkout page, upsells etc
(yes you want to keep upsells in place for this).
Like I said, this example is for a supplement, but you can literally do this
same thing for an info-product as well.
Just make sure you put some type of header on the page that lets them
know it’s a special sale.
Now in terms of the sale, you don’t even have to discount stuff, but you do
need to make a more compelling offer than your typical one.
So for Patriot Power Greens instead of offering 3 canisters of the
supplement, we’re going to stack some special bonuses on top of the offer
that are really valuable (note: you want bonuses that people REALLY want,
not just free reports).
So a couple examples we used in the past when I ran these were…
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 30 --

- A free shaker bottle
- 7 mini travel packs of Patriot Power Greens
- Protein packs
- A canister of a similar product - Patriot Reds
My old business partner Allen in his survival business has used bonuses
like…
- Handheld water purifier
- Swiss army knife
- Survival seeds
- Commemorative coins ( a Trump coin would work great for that
market)
I personally like stacking really appealing bonuses on like this because it’s
cheaper than giving a discount on the price. And I think it’s actually more
appealing than a price reduction as well.
(Although if you have a lot of margin like with a high-priced info product,
you could also discount your product it if you want).
Honestly, don’t get too hung up on this part, cause it really doesn’t make a
huge difference which way you go.
Just offer some really good bonuses, or a discount and you’re good to go.
Now for how to promote this thing…
Like I said this is all done via your email list. The bigger/more responsive
your email list, the better this will work.
Overall you’re going to send 10 emails to your list.
Day 1 - 2 emails
Day 2 - 2 emails
Day 3 - 2 emails (St Patty’s Day)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 30 --

Day 4 - 4 emails (sale ends at midnight PT on day 4)
Each day you send one email in the morning (like 7-8 AM) and then one in
the evening (like 5-6 PM).
On Day 4, you’ll send them like this…
First email - 7 AM
Second email - 5 PM
Third email - 8 PM
Last email - 11 PM (one hour to go!)
That’s it.
Now in terms of what emails you send them, here’s a few examples of how
I did this for our greens supplement.
Note: You’ll see a section in here
Email #1 on Day 1

your email today...

line of copy at the top.

So those are the Day 2 emails. As you can see they are all about sharing
success stories.
Why?
Because success stories = proof. Nothing persuades more than proof.
And no form of proof is more powerful than success stories. They key here
is that the success stories match the people you’re selling to (i.e. if you sell
to 50 year old women, don’t use success stories of a 25 yr old man)
You want the stories to be relatable.
And the more “zero to hero” they are the better. So if someone goes from
having one foot in the grave and their doctor telling them “it’s over” - but 3
months later they beat the odds and they’re running in the Boston
marathon - that’s a hell of a story.
That’s a big change.
So you want to look for stories that have big changes in them
If you don’t have these, you make do with what you have.
And find the best one you have.
Make sense?
Alright, so now we have two days of emails done. At this point we are
halfway home. So let’s dive into the 3rd day of emails.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 16 of 30 --

Again, on the 3rd day we’re going to hit on more success stories. And
we’re also going to answer any objections that people might have.
Day 3 - Email #1
Subj: Vietnam Vet finds “new life” with this 6 oz drink
When Tom first ordered our Patriot Power Greens supplement his
body was falling apart…
He was tired all the time…
His back ached.
Years and years of punishment in the Vietnam war, and just everyday
life had taken it’s toll on Tom.
But then something amazing happened…
He got his “mojo” back.
I’ll let Tom tell you the story.
"I got my 7 packets of Patriot Power Greens a couple days ago, and
started taking them yesterday.
"I am a 66-year-old veteran of the US Army. I served 18 months in
Vietnam.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 17 of 30 --

"I was pretty active up until I injured my back and neck in 1995. Even
after surgery, which made me feel worse than when I went in, I
managed to be able to work at a desk. I was ambulatory...
"In 2007, something happened and I found out I was a victim of
Agent Orange.
"I became severely obese, had swelling in my legs and ankles, lost
energy and strength, and basically became a couch potato. I am now
100% disabled with the VA, and collect disability from Social
Security.
"In 2012, I had elective surgery to remove my stomach (bariatric
sleeve). I have been able to keep the 75 lbs. I lost off ever since, but
still have no energy, walk around in a 'fog,' and for the past year, I
have had difficulty eating regular food.
"The smell of food cooking would make me nauseous, and often, I
would vomit after eating.
"The only things I could tolerate were protein bars and shakes. I have
been trying all kinds of other things in an attempt to restore my
digestive system, with little success.
"Like I said earlier, I started my samples of Patriot Power Greens
yesterday. I was able to stay awake and work at my computer all
morning.
"Then, in the afternoon my wife and I went out to upgrade our cell
phones. We were out for several hours. After taking care of our cell
phones we went grocery shopping. We ended up getting home
around 6pm.
"After all this I still had energy to put groceries away. I must admit, I
was wiped-out after that...
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 18 of 30 --

"My point is this: after taking Patriot Power Greens first thing in the
morning, I was 'awake' all day, and had energy to go out and be
almost 'human' the entire day.
"Quite a difference from what I have been experiencing. I feel pretty
good today, and am writing this email to you. I haven't put this much
energy into anything in quite some time.
"Thank you for introducing me to Patriot Power Greens."
Wow.
What a story.
I can only imagine what it must have felt like for Tom.
He'd given his all for his country, and now his body was falling apart.
And I feel so proud to be able to play a little part in helping him regain
some of his health.
That’s what Patriot Power Greens does…
It lets you move and feel like you did 15-20 years ago.

Day 3 - Email #2
Subj: Is Patriot Power Greens a SCAM?
One of the common questions I get is people wondering whether or
not Patriot Power Greens is a scam.
To me this is a weird question.
Would elite military units in the U.S. Coast Guard being using it if it
was a scam?
Would Dr. Sebring, the creator of Patriot Power Greens, be staking his
name, his medical practice and his national reputation on this if it was
a scam?
My guess is probably not.
Cause here’s the truth...
Since we released Patriot Power Greens in March of 2014, over
321,479 men and women (just like you) have tried Patriot Power
Greens.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Their thoughts?
Tony S. from Nashville said “my knees were beat up from being a
plumber for 25 years. After a few weeks of your greens, they aren’t
creaking like they normally do”
John R. from Birmingham said “my wife is 82 and was having trouble
getting up and down the stairs the last few months. I ordered PPG in
April, and we’ve been taking it every morning. Now she’s getting up
and down the stairs with no problem.”
Cindy J. from San Diego said “after three years of doing nothing
exercise wise, my daughter finally convinced me to start walking with
her every night. At first, I could barely make it around the block
(about 5 minutes), but now we’re doing 45 minutes together every
night. I can’t thank you enough for the patriot greens, it’s giving me
the energy to stay active and at my age this is a blessing”
Honestly, I could show you a whole page of success stories like this.
I have folders filled with them in my office.
Point is, Patriot Power Greens flat out works.
Most people start to see results in as little as 3 days.
Imagine waking up in the morning and not aching all over…

you didn’t have before.
Or how about simply getting around like you did 10-15 years ago?
That’s what Patriot Power Greens helps you do.

-----
So to recap Day 3, we hit them with more success stories, and we also
answered common objections (i.e. is Patriot Power Greens a scam?)
Now we’re moving on to the last day, day 4.
Day 4 is a big one because most people wait til the very last minute to
buy things.
One thing I want to emphasize, it’s crucial that you send a bunch of
emails to end the day.
I’ve tested doing this and not doing it. Everytime I send more emails,
I make WAYYYYY more sales.
I recommend 4 emails on the last day, and then 3 of those will be in
the evening.
So it will look like this…
First email - 7 AM
Second email - 5 PM
Third email - 8 PM
Last email - 11 PM (one hour to go!)
Let’s jump into Day 4 emails…
-------
Day 4 - Email #1
From: Karen Reagan (note we change this to Jeff’s wife)
Subj: Re: your order (note this subject line works very well, but you will
also get a lot of confused replies from people thinking they ordered
something)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Day 4 - Email #2
Subj: Why you should NOT buy Patriot Power Greens
If you’re wondering whether or not you should get Patriot Power Greens,
here’s a few reasons you should NOT buy it…
1. You already feel like Superman when you wake up everyday
2. You have the non-stop energy of a toddler after a sugar-rush
3. You’re content with your situation and not worried about looking,
feeling or moving better
If any of those 3 reasons describe you, then I would suggest you don’t buy
Patriot Power Greens.

Notes & Important Points To Remember
When we did these promos we did one every single month. Basically use
common holidays to have sales - Veterans Day, 4th of July, Memorial Day,
St Patty’s Day.
You can also use your birthday, the day your company was founded etc…
You can also do specialty holidays for your niche - like if you sell tax stuff to
business owners, you could run a promo around all 4 of the major tax days
when business owners pay their estimated taxes.
Or if you’re in the relationship niche, leading up to Valentine’s day would be
a great time to have a sale.
There’s a million ideas you can use. You just have to have a REASON for
having a sale - and a holiday is a great reason for that.
Also…
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 28 of 30 --

When I do these emails, I keep them very simple. I just send them to
everyone on the list. So if someone buys on day 1, they aren’t moved into
some other sequence getting different emails.
They keep getting the emails even though they bought. Is this the best way
to do it? Probably not. But I’m a fan of keeping things simple.
And like I said, these holiday sales added about $2 million in profit to our
business over the course of a year.
So you can do the same thing with any of your courses, products, whatver.
The key is that you simply DO THEM.
You can get a LOT of this stuff wrong that I just told you about, and it will
still work really damn well. So that’s nice.
Notes for freelance copywriters
If you’re a freelancer reading this, this is a great campaign that you can do
for a client.
Don’t try to sell them right off the bat on doing one every month.
Just sell them on doing the first one.
An easy way to propose it would be to say you’ll do it on a performance
basis, and you want 10%-20% of the net revenue.
So if the promo makes $200,000 in net revenue - at 10%, you get $20,000.
That’s a good deal for you. And for the business owner.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 29 of 30 --

Then once that one goes well, tell them you want to do another one for the
next holiday coming up.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
